notes: Self-correction iteration: within ONE turn pi runs a tool call that fails, interprets the failure, and retries with corrected input — multiple tool-call/result cycles back to back — and the whole arc stays a SINGLE contiguous working span with no flicker to ready or waiting between attempts, settling to ready only at the single turn end. For pi, a turn ends only when the final assistant message carries stopReason=stop; intermediate toolCall/toolResult pairs (including a FAILING toolResult) do not close the turn, so the multi-step self-correction is one ready→working→ready arc — exactly one turn_end ready, no intermediate ready, no waiting. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE pi driver (drive-pi-interactive.sh, a tmux REPL), NOT headless `pi --print`. WHY interactive: headless `pi --print` exits the instant it emits the final reply, so the daemon can miss the terminal working→ready settle before process_exited (concern C6); keeping pi alive in the REPL across one send + wait_turn lets the daemon observe the settle cleanly, and the single tmux teardown then yields one clean process_exited. The prompt forces a failing shell call (`cat` of a nonexistent file) followed by a corrective call (`ls`) in the same turn, so a real failing toolResult sits inside the working span and the parser must hold the session working across it. C7 / session-identity: the live REPL emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch (it bridges the gap until the UUID-keyed rollout transcript appears), so session_birth — which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch' — legitimately matches that pre-session (same shape as pi/auto-executed-tool-call and pi/multi-turn-conversation). The TURN arc, by contrast, must land on the UUID rollout session: turn_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording, which is necessarily the rollout session (the pre-session never goes working); turn_end, pid_bind, and teardown then all pin to turn_start's session via same_session_as, so a co-occurring pre-session can never satisfy them. The failing-then-recovery tool calls are observable in transcript.jsonl as pi toolCall / toolResult message events (parser.go); the phase DSL has no tool-event form, so the retry cycle is asserted via the single sustained working span plus the transcript pairs, not a dedicated phase. HOW runs differ: exact assistant wording, the tool's error text, per-turn token counts, and sub-second timing vary (model/OS-dependent). Stable structurally: one ready→working→ready arc on one rollout session_id, multiple toolCall/toolResult pairs (at least one failing) inside the single working span, a single process_exited, and a final ready state.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch
Prompt requiring tool use → working (binds the rollout session; the pre-session never enters working)

State stays continuously working across the failing call and its corrective retry, then settles to ready at the single turn end — no flicker to waiting between attempts (the FIRST ready after turn_start is this terminal one, proving no intermediate ready flicker)
PID bound to the rollout session
REPL torn down once after the turn → a single process_exited on the rollout session; state immediately before teardown is ready
